item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The screamers ruin it for me, they jump out of nowhere and take a huge dump over an otherwise nice and interesting article. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are you a masochist?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's literally the worst SCP on the site.

I liked it though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please don't inflict this on the French people. They don't deserve this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How cruel with yourself (>_<). In fact, I'm translating it for the french branch.
Edit : It's done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More importantly, why is anyone reading this? It's literally the worst SCP on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But why? Why is it better than the original?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tracked Probe 2173-07 was equipped with infrared, ultraviolet, and visible spectrum monitoring equipment.

Tracked Probe 2173-07 was equipped with ultraviolet, infrared, and visible spectrum monitoring equipment.

I took the liberty of editing this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, there's something nagging me about the "pattern screamers".

What have they done to force our acknowledgement, other than show up in errors?

This isn't minor. This is a set of entities over the things that we consider to be the outcome of inherently chaotic processes.

Moreover, these Pattern Screamers are capable of creating the disorder itself in otherwise orderly systems, without any physical contact.

If you emphasized this aspect of them, I think they would be a lot stronger than they are now.

Anyway, they've made a curious mistake in their contact of humanity, they expected us to be smarter and better at connecting seemingly unrelated dots than we are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They look like their author is going to delete them so don't get attached.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agent MacLeod, what is appearance of 2173-1? Do they look like humanoids, reptiloids, oods (Dr Who), or something else?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most of the things I want to say have been pointed out so far - While the story is elaborate and interesting I cannot help but feel the appearance of Pattern Screamers to be absurd. You could remove it once and for all, or you could maybe be build a smoother connection that leads to it. Or maybe a stronger implication in the mid part that 3rd party is involved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Never saw this post until now, but I was aware of this, yes. I have no idea who you are, OP, but I'm sorry I didn't get 'round to acknowledging you sooner.

The existence of this fanart presents a conundrum for me, because I really, really want to delete this when I have a rewrite ready. Eugh. This pains me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mac, are you aware that you have fanart?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yep. I did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hunted through the page source, but didn't find any text I didn't see. Maybe I read the wrong revision.

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST- CALL FOR REVIEW
item.content:encoded:
No, I'm still listening, I'm still here.

I know, the whole thing is kinda screwy. I have a rewrite in the works that ought to address that problem.

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST- CALL FOR REVIEW
item.content:encoded:
Don't know if you'll see this, but here's my critique, and it's simple:

There is no link between the overlapping worlds and the Pattern Screamers. No cause-and-effect: no reason to believe the Screamers are natives, nothing to attract their presence, just BAM genocidal angry things appear. It feels like two half-created articles slapped together with no effort to really make the link.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just saying that imaginary places kind of inconvenience my map project. I've only been able to mark locations for about 1/5th of the SCPs I've covered already. The rest are either:

- In fictional places
- In redacted locations or just don't have their locations mentioned at all
- Too vague/nonspecific with their locations (no specific city/county/region listed)
- Aren't even on Earth
- Have no specific location

So I like to leave these comments in hope that the writer it others will point out some clues or other information I missed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. That's the point. Someone who's new to the community and still isn't convinced this stuff is fake looks it up, and then holy shit, the Foundation must have deleted it from the records! Plus, I don't like redacting info, but I don't like using real places, so I make names up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No such place as Dellmarsh, Maine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On 6 October, 1999

Hey that's my first birthday.

But this is really good. +1 from me.

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST- CALL FOR REVIEW
item.content:encoded:
First off, could someone please go back and edit out the part where hidden sections are mentioned? I read it and copied the entire article onto my Sandbox page to make sure I wasnt missing anything before I noticed the comment saying that hidden text has been removed :P

I'll be honest and say I dont know where this is going. The Pattern Screamers are unfamiliar to me, but the SCP itself seems a little dull. Its a dome where another planet over laps ours in a specific area… Oh and we cant go into it. Oh and theres an army in there.. Except that now they've left so its just a weird environment now.

I assume a lot of this hinges on the Pattern Scanners reference but this just seems like two war like cultures meeting each other for the first time and being understandably nervous. The changed environment is interesting, but if North Korea became covered in noxious fog and the people wore gas masks you could write a similar SCP based on that.

In short I'm not sure its unique enough and the depth of detail just seems to show it up. If the Pattern Scanners reference is so crucial to the udnerstanding of it then I'm not sure the article can stand up for itself.

If I've misunderstood anything just let me know, I think its an interesting premise but you could do so much more with it. I think I might give it a go myself at some point, I do like the idea of different worlds overlapping.

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST- CALL FOR REVIEW
item.content:encoded: It's not that i didn't like it, however i didn't find the dome and the aliens behind it attractive, and the screamers confused it all. I did like the addenum, a scientist tired of foundation bullshit, so i will upvote that :)

item.title: AUTHOR POST- CALL FOR REVIEW
item.content:encoded:
NOTE: I AM NOT STAFF, NOR AM I ATTEMPTING TO IMITATE STAFF. THIS IS BEING POSTED EXCLUSIVELY IN MY CAPACITY AS THE AUTHOR OF THIS PIECE.

This is not doing as well as I'd hoped, but not so poorly that I feel inclined to self-delete. I know that this can be improved upon. I am asking anyone who did not like this SCP or felt more could be done with it to provide input as to how to improve this.
Also, does anyone know AstronautJoe IRL? I'd like to work more closely with him on my future Pattern Screamer endeavors.
Staff, if I am in any way breaking rules/being a general pest, I will edit this post so as to be within site rules.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Ok, so, because of the "meh" reception this has had so far, I've made a major stylistic edit: There is now no hidden text. You were once blind, but now you see. :D Huge thanks to TwistedGears for encouraging that original path before I posted, and to Zyn for her words of wisdom here. Please, if you read this before, reread and reconsider your rate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have no idea how much this means to me— to me, negative feedback is actually more important than positive feedback. It tells me what to fix, what to avoid, and what is acceptable. A large number of people have downvoted this without clarifying why (not that they have to, but their input would be useful) except for "It's too similar to 'x'." I don't know who started the Pattern Screamer shtick, but I would've asked for permission/feedback if I did. I don't think it's too out-of-character for them, and I was actually trying to tone down the narm found in SCP-S, but everyone has their own interpretation and sometimes, an author will write something incompatible with someone else's headcanon. Nothing to do to avoid that, I suppose.

As far as the name thing is concerned, it's going away soon.

Again, thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I must admit, I had a heart attack both when I saw the hidden collapsible and at the first mention of the Pattern Screamer. The skip itself is written pretty well, and I have to offer you props for your first successful article being one this ambitious.

However, I can't in good conscience upvote. My reasoning is the last note. For a start, it seems… kinda overblown to me, and waaaaaay too melodramatic. Moreover, I can't say I'm fond of the Pattern Screamer's characterisation here. I like the idea of minor glitches and errors in Foundation software being indicative of it/them, but this just makes it seem… pathetic, for want of a better world. Genocidal maniacs don't seem like the sort to go the "please notice me senpai" route. Well, they can be, but in this case, it doesn't fit. Furthermore, this is far more personal, but I'm kinda discomforted that you're advancing the mythos this far, given that the original author's stated before that he intends to write a Pattern Screamer SCP.

Also, real nitpicky here, but the exclamation mark in the "Welcome [name here]!" bit feels way too advert-y to me.

Overall, it's a good effort, and I can't downvote it, but I'm not upvoting either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Invisible? Yes. Hard to find? No. If I wanted it to be hard to find, the code would've looked like this:

 [[collapsible show=" " hide="-Incident 2173-12"]]

Instead of this: 
 [[collapsible show=" " hide="-Incident 2173-12"]]

Also, the first collapsible is equally hard to find ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Recovery: On 02/09/1998, Global Occult Coalition operatives carried out a raid on a Chaos Insurgency facility several kilometers north of Dellmarsh, Maine. Foundation agents in the area were acting as escorts to the GOC convoy. The raid was successful, with no critical GOC casualties.
Several anomalous items were recovered by the GOC, including [REDACTED]. Also among the recovered items was an interdimensional portal (tentatively designated SCP-███ pre-recovery) being operated by the Chaos Insurgency as a means of communicating with SCP-2173-1, with the suspected intentions of securing anomalous weaponry.

Shouldn't there be a paragraph break after "casualties?" I think there's one, but you forgot to double-space it like the others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good work hiding that last collapsible.

The question, then, is, "Are you sure you've read EVERY collapsible?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good work hiding that last collapsible. That said, I feel like I've seen this idea before, both "hazardous contained zone filled with stuff what can live there" and "GoI negotiates with skip". Overall, it didn't really catch my attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I was afraid of UTD comparisons, but I don't think it's all that similar. Under the Dome takes place in southern Maine, while this is in northern Maine. This happened because of a fuckup with an interdimensional portal, while UTD is Lovecraftian children treating people like toys. The Dome in UTD is like the Dome in the Simpsons Movie, while this is like an intangible semi-permeable membrane. Plus, the people in UTD didn't suffocate instantly.
Fun fact: the only Stephen King stories I've read are Under the Dome and The Stand, although I've seen the film adaptation of The Mist. All other resemblance is coincidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a healthy mix of Dome, Tommyknockers, and The Mist (oopsie-portal to another dimension that ends up leaking in), but I think it hangs together pretty well. The initial "it's a dome over a town in Maine" gave me a lot of pause, though, and I still wish it wasn't quite so 1-to-1 with UTD. I see this one as a love letter to King, even if it wasn't meant that way.

I also think the skip is stronger without the Recovered Document, too, which I didn't expect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm downvoting. Too similar to Stephen King's "Under The Dome"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
08/21-07/31/1998

It appears that one of the other effects of this SCP is making time move backwards. :I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, how did I help with coding? I'm shit at coding. >.>
But overall, this article has a nice buildup, the item is generally interesting, and the hidden collapsibles are a nice touch, so I'll upvote.
E: > 00 (that's my nickname for the dude, I forget his username) His username is projectzerozero.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That recovery log seems very familiar to me.

+ Also
- Also,

is that last collapsible supposed to be hard to find?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Article's alright, but the dome and the people inside the dome reminds me of Stephen King's The Tommyknockers, while the other events going on in town are reminiscent of SCP-1936.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of that monorail spoonofevil wrote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found the secrets collapsibles yay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun fact: You can find out what's behind the [REDACTED] if you look hard enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The buildup of tension is pretty damn good here, and for once redacting the final event in a timeline that triggers some sort of bizarre occurrence didn't completely and utterly turn me off. Kudos.
also you realize now that if Djoric wants to rewrite 173 he won't be able to do it anymore you monster

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
08/04/1998: Several distressed communiques from SCP-2173-1 indicate alarm at Containment Site 2173's 4th of July preparations. It

The 4th of July is 07/04. 08/04 is August 4th.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, huge thank you to TwistedGears, who, though I'm pretty sure he'll downvote, made this damn thing possible by pointing out some really stupid shit at every stage in the drafting process. Another thanks goes to Zolgamax, who gave me the confidence to post this thing.

Other people who have looked at this in the forums and chat are as follows(Don't be surprised if you see your name; I've been throwing this motherfucker at chat for awhile):
Dr Leonard
Petrograd
Jacob Conwell
alenny
Riemann
LordSpy
Bland
00 (that's my nickname for the dude, I forget his username)
Choiwel
Waterfire
Fluke
TroyL, Crayne, Aelanna, Reject, and Live2Die for stuff on coding
Omegatronacles
Accelerando
Montala (sort of)

Ok, that's everyone (I think). Reply to this if you helped at any point and want recognition, but haven't gotten it yet.
